methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $35M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Array Labs, a Series A-stage space and aerospace company led by Andrew Peterson and Jose Isaac Robledo, has raised $35M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparables universe of 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs.

Of three triangulation methods examined, only stage-progression analysis proved applicable. This method uses the company's $35M total capital raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement to Series B, yielding $70M. Peer-median analysis was not executable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables in the narrow Space & Aerospace peer set (only 2 adjacent-stage datapoints available against a minimum threshold of 3). Public-comp anchoring, while supported by a Space & Aerospace median P/S of 25.8x across the 5 public tickers, could not be applied because Array Labs' revenue remains undisclosed, as is standard for private companies at this stage.

The single-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $60M to $80M, with a median estimate of $70M. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue data needed for comparable-company multiples analysis and limited transaction precedent at the exact stage and valuation tier. The high sensitivity to the 2.0x stage-progression multiplier underscores the importance of revenue visibility in future fundraising rounds.
